subject: Public Health
title: Guinea Worm: Near Eradication
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Guinea Worm: Near Eradication.

hook.text: A meter-long worm burrows out of your leg over weeks—excruciatingly painful and completely preventable.

buildup.text: Guinea worm larvae live in water fleas—swallowed in unfiltered drinking water from stagnant ponds.

discovery.text: There's no vaccine and no medicine. Prevention relies entirely on filtering water through cloth.

twist.text: From 3.5 million cases in 1986, guinea worm has been reduced to just 13 cases in 2023—without any drug.

climax.text: It will be only the second human disease eradicated—and the first without a vaccine.

punchline.text: A cloth filter is defeating a parasite that plagued humanity for millennia.

quiz.question: How many guinea worm cases remained in 2023?
